# imprint-pdf — Full LLM Context

> Author PDFs as React components, styled with real Tailwind classes. One `pdf()` function. No Chromium. Runs on Node, Bun, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge, and the browser. Apache-2.0.

This file is a single-shot LLM context: install, API, framework wiring, common patterns, error → fix, type signatures. For a navigable index, see [`llms.txt`](https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/imprint-pdf/blob/main/llms.txt).

---

## Install

```bash
pnpm add @imprint-pdf/react @imprint-pdf/core tailwindcss
pnpm add -D @imprint-pdf/cli
npx imprint init
```

Current alpha (under the `alpha` dist-tag, not `latest`): `@imprint-pdf/core@1.0.0-alpha.7`, `@imprint-pdf/react@1.0.0-alpha.8`, `@imprint-pdf/next@1.0.0-alpha.8`, `@imprint-pdf/fonts@1.0.0-alpha.6`. Pin explicitly while we're pre-1.0 — `latest` may lag.

`imprint init` detects Next.js / Vite / generic Node and wires the rest.

Framework-specific (alongside the core install):

```bash
pnpm add @imprint-pdf/next        # Next.js (App or Pages Router, any runtime)
pnpm add -D @imprint-pdf/vite     # Vite (SPA, SSR, Vike, Astro)
```

Cloudflare Workers / Bun don't need a dedicated package — they import `@imprint-pdf/react/standalone` directly.

### Required peers

- React 18.2+ or 19.x (both reconciler majors bundled, picked at module load)
- Tailwind CSS v3.4+ or v4.x (auto-detected from installed version)
- Node 20+ (or Bun ≥ 1.0, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge)
- `postcss` peer required only when using Tailwind v3

### Optional add-ons

```bash
pnpm add @imprint-pdf/print     # PDF/X-4, CMYK, ICC, PDF/A, factur-X
pnpm add @imprint-pdf/sign      # PKCS#7 signing
pnpm add @imprint-pdf/ua        # PDF/UA-1 tagged PDF / accessibility
pnpm add -D @imprint-pdf/eslint # Lint rules
```

---

## The whole API

```ts
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/react';

// Response (default) — for web framework routes
pdf(element: ReactElement, options?: PdfOptions & { as?: 'response' }): Promise<Response>;

// Uint8Array — for writing to disk, signing, attaching to email
pdf(element: ReactElement, options: PdfOptions & { as: 'bytes' }): Promise<Uint8Array>;

// ReadableStream<Uint8Array> — for streaming on edge runtimes
pdf(element: ReactElement, options: PdfOptions & { as: 'stream' }): Promise<ReadableStream<Uint8Array>>;
```

`pdf()` overloads narrow the return type by the literal value of `options.as`. The default is `'response'`.

### `PdfOptions`

```ts
interface PdfOptions extends RenderOptions {
  /** Output shape — default 'response' */
  as?: 'response' | 'bytes' | 'stream';
  /** Content-Disposition filename (response shape only) */
  filename?: string;
  /** 'inline' (default) or 'attachment' */
  disposition?: 'inline' | 'attachment';
}

interface RenderOptions {
  fonts?: FontDeclaration[];
  tailwind?: {
    config?: string;     // path to tailwind.config.{ts,js,mjs,cjs} (v3)
    stylesheet?: string; // path to v4 CSS entry
    projectRoot?: string;
  };
  assetResolver?: AssetResolver;
  postProcess?: PdfPostProcessHook[];  // for @imprint-pdf/print, @imprint-pdf/ua
  postBytes?: PdfPostBytesHook[];      // for @imprint-pdf/sign
  hyphenate?: (word: string) => string[];
  svgRasterizer?: SvgRasterizer;
  debug?: boolean;
}
```

`pdf()` auto-loads `imprint.config.ts` from the project root and merges caller-supplied options on top.

### Three common output shapes

```ts
// Next.js / Hono / Bun route — returns a Response
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/next';
export const GET = () => pdf(<Invoice data={data} />);

// Write to disk — returns Uint8Array
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/react';
import { writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
const bytes = await pdf(<Invoice data={data} />, { as: 'bytes' });
writeFileSync('./invoice.pdf', bytes);

// Streaming (large reports, low memory) — returns ReadableStream
const stream = await pdf(<Report data={data} />, { as: 'stream' });
return new Response(stream, { headers: { 'content-type': 'application/pdf' } });
```

---

## Configuration

`imprint.config.ts` lives at the project root. Every field is optional.

```ts
import { defineConfig } from '@imprint-pdf/core/config';

export default defineConfig({
  fonts: [
    { family: 'Inter', src: './public/fonts/Inter.woff2' },
    { family: 'Inter', src: './public/fonts/Inter-Bold.woff2', weight: 700 },
    {
      family: 'Inter Variable',
      src: './public/fonts/InterVariable.woff2',
      variable: true,
      axes: { wght: [100, 900] },
    },
  ],
  tailwind: {
    // Either is fine; auto-detection covers most setups.
    stylesheet: './src/app.css',          // → Tailwind v4
    // config: './tailwind.config.ts',    // → Tailwind v3
  },
  typography: {
    lineBreaking: 'knuth-plass',          // or 'greedy'
    hyphenation: { en: true, de: true },
    defaultWidows: 2,
    defaultOrphans: 2,
    subset: true,
  },
  output: {
    intent: 'PDF/X-4',                    // requires @imprint-pdf/print
    outputIntent: { profile: 'FOGRA39' },
    compress: true,
    version: '1.7',
  },
  assets: {
    baseUrl: 'https://cdn.acme.com',
    cacheDir: './.imprint-cache',
  },
  outDir: 'out',
  debug: false,
});
```

Auto-detected stylesheet paths (first match wins): `src/app.css`, `src/globals.css`, `src/index.css`, `src/styles.css`, `src/styles/app.css`, `src/styles/globals.css`, `app/globals.css`, `app/app.css`, `styles/app.css`, `styles/globals.css`.

---

## Components

### Document chrome

```tsx
import {
  Document, Page, Header, Footer, Watermark, Bookmark,
  PageNumber, TotalPages,
} from '@imprint-pdf/react';

<Document title="Q1 Report" author="Acme" lang="en">
  <Header>
    <div className="text-xs text-gray-500">Acme — Confidential</div>
  </Header>
  <Footer className="flex justify-between text-xs">
    <span>Q1 2026</span>
    <span>Page <PageNumber /> of <TotalPages /></span>
  </Footer>
  <Watermark className="text-9xl font-bold text-gray-100">DRAFT</Watermark>

  <Page size="A4" orientation="portrait" className="p-12 font-sans">
    <Bookmark title="Cover" />
    <h1 className="text-4xl font-bold">Quarterly Report</h1>
  </Page>
</Document>
```

`<PageNumber>` and `<TotalPages>` resolve at draw time. They're inline-flex by default so they work as siblings of text.

### Content (HTML)

Plain HTML elements emit `PdfNode` types with semantic role tags. PDF/UA tagging works automatically.

```tsx
<div className="flex flex-row gap-4">
  <h1 className="text-3xl font-bold">Title</h1>
  <p className="text-base leading-relaxed">Body copy.</p>
  <ul><li>Item</li></ul>
  <table><tr><td>cell</td></tr></table>
</div>
```

Recognised: `<div>`, `<span>`, `<p>`, `<h1>`–`<h6>`, `<ul>`, `<ol>`, `<li>`, `<table>`, `<thead>`, `<tbody>`, `<tfoot>`, `<tr>`, `<td>`, `<th>`, `<a>`, `<img>`, `<section>`, `<article>`, `<aside>`, `<header>`, `<footer>`, `<main>`, `<nav>`, `<figure>`, `<figcaption>`, `<blockquote>`, `<pre>`, `<code>`, `<strong>`, `<em>`, `<small>`, `<label>`.

### Imprint-specific content

```tsx
import { Image, Svg, Link, Chart } from '@imprint-pdf/react';

<Image src="./logo.png" alt="Acme logo" className="h-10 w-auto" />
<Svg src="<svg viewBox='0 0 24 24'>…</svg>" className="h-8 w-8" />
<Link href="#section-2" className="text-blue-600 underline">Jump</Link>
<Chart src="<svg>...</svg>" /* Recharts/Visx/ECharts SVG output */ />
```

### Forms (AcroForms)

```tsx
import {
  Form, TextField, Checkbox, RadioGroup, Dropdown,
  Signature, Button,
} from '@imprint-pdf/react';

<Form>
  <TextField name="fullname" required />
  <TextField name="email" type="email" required />
  <Checkbox name="terms" required />
  <RadioGroup name="plan" options={[
    { value: 'pro', label: 'Pro' },
    { value: 'team', label: 'Team' },
  ]} />
  <Dropdown name="country" options={[/* … */]} />
  <Signature name="sign1" />
  <Button name="submit" action="SubmitForm" />
</Form>
```

---

## Framework setup

### Next.js (App Router)

```ts
// next.config.ts
import { withImprint } from '@imprint-pdf/next/plugin';

export default withImprint()({
  output: 'standalone',  // for Docker / Coolify / self-hosted Vercel
  // …rest of your Next config
});
```

`withImprint()` is required for production deploys (see [Standalone deployments](#standalone-deployments-docker--coolify--self-hosted-vercel) below). It:

1. Registers the webpack plugin for compile-time Tailwind class extraction (webpack only — Turbopack falls back to runtime compile).
2. Enables `asyncWebAssembly` + `layers` webpack experiments and the `webassembly/async` rule for `.wasm`.
3. Adds `@imprint-pdf/{react,core,next}` to `serverExternalPackages` (Next 15) **and** `experimental.serverComponentsExternalPackages` (Next 14).
4. Adds `tailwindcss` + `postcss` to the same externals **and** writes `experimental.outputFileTracingIncludes` globs so Vercel's [node-file-tracer](https://github.com/vercel/nft) copies them into `.next/standalone/node_modules` during `next build`.

```ts
// app/api/invoice/[id]/route.ts
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/next';
import { Invoice } from '@/templates/Invoice';

export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
// export const runtime = 'edge';  // ← auto-detected; opt in if you want edge

export async function GET(_req: Request, { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }) {
  const { id } = await params;
  const data = await fetchInvoice(id);
  return pdf(<Invoice data={data} />, { filename: `invoice-${id}.pdf` });
}
```

`pdf()` from `@imprint-pdf/next` auto-detects edge vs Node via `NEXT_RUNTIME` / `globalThis.EdgeRuntime` and dispatches to the right `@imprint-pdf/react` build.

#### Standalone deployments (Docker / Coolify / self-hosted Vercel)

`next build` with `output: 'standalone'` runs nft across each route's import graph and copies only the matched files into `.next/standalone/node_modules`. imprint-pdf has three things that nft can't statically follow and that `withImprint()` makes visible to it:

- The matching `react-reconciler` for your React major (R18 lazy-loaded via `await import('react-reconciler-18')`, R19 via `-19`).
- The consumer's `tailwindcss` and `postcss` for v3 Tailwind compilation (resolved via `createRequire(projectRoot)` at render time).
- The consumer's `tailwind.config.{ts,js,mjs,cjs}` for v3 dispatch.

Use `withImprint()` (≥ `@imprint-pdf/next@1.0.0-alpha.8`) and `@imprint-pdf/react@1.0.0-alpha.7+`. If you can't drop `withImprint()` into your `next.config`, set the same keys manually:

```js
// next.config.{js,mjs,ts}
const nextConfig = {
  output: 'standalone',

  serverExternalPackages: [
    '@imprint-pdf/react', '@imprint-pdf/core', '@imprint-pdf/next',
    'tailwindcss', 'postcss',
  ],

  experimental: {
    // Next 14 — Next 15+ reads the top-level key above.
    serverComponentsExternalPackages: [
      '@imprint-pdf/react', '@imprint-pdf/core', '@imprint-pdf/next',
      'tailwindcss', 'postcss',
    ],
    outputFileTracingIncludes: {
      '**/*': [
        './node_modules/tailwindcss/**/*',
        './node_modules/postcss/**/*',
        './node_modules/.pnpm/tailwindcss@*/node_modules/tailwindcss/**/*',
        './node_modules/.pnpm/postcss@*/node_modules/postcss/**/*',
      ],
    },
  },
};

export default nextConfig;
```

Alternative: move `tailwindcss` + `postcss` from `devDependencies` to `dependencies` in your app's `package.json` (nft only traces dependencies by default).

Verify before deploying:

```bash
pnpm next build
node .next/standalone/server.js &
curl -i http://localhost:3000/api/invoice    # → 200, %PDF-…
```

### Vite

```ts
// vite.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react';
import { imprint } from '@imprint-pdf/vite';

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react(), imprint()],
});
```

```ts
// src/App.tsx
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/react';

async function download() {
  const response = await pdf(<Invoice data={data} />);
  window.open(URL.createObjectURL(await response.blob()));
}
```

### Bun

```ts
// src/index.ts
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/react';
import { Invoice } from './templates/Invoice';

Bun.serve({
  port: 3000,
  fetch: (req) => pdf(<Invoice data={fetchInvoice(req)} />),
});
```

### Cloudflare Workers

```ts
// src/index.ts
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/react/standalone';
import wasm from '@imprint-pdf/react/imprint.wasm';
import { Invoice } from './templates/Invoice';

export default {
  fetch: () => pdf(<Invoice data={data} />, { wasm }),
};
```

```toml
# wrangler.toml
[wasm_modules]
IMPRINT_WASM = "./node_modules/@imprint-pdf/react/dist/imprint.wasm"
```

---

## Tailwind

imprint-pdf runs the actual Tailwind compiler. Plugins, `@theme` tokens, arbitrary values, OKLCH colors all work. Auto-detects v3 vs v4 from the consumer's installed `tailwindcss`.

### v4 (CSS-first, recommended)

```css
/* src/app.css */
@import 'tailwindcss';
@import '@imprint-pdf/react/preset';

@theme {
  --font-sans: 'Inter', sans-serif;
  --color-brand-500: #0ea5e9;
}
```

The `@imprint-pdf/react/preset` import registers the page-position variants (`page-first:`, `page-left:`, `page-right:`) so Tailwind knows about them.

### v3 (JS config)

```ts
// tailwind.config.ts
import type { Config } from 'tailwindcss';
export default {
  content: ['./src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'],
  theme: { extend: { /* … */ } },
} satisfies Config;
```

```ts
// imprint.config.ts
export default defineConfig({
  tailwind: { config: './tailwind.config.ts' },
});
```

### Imprint-specific variants

| Variant                        | What it does                                            |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `page-first:`                  | Applies only on the first page                          |
| `page-left:`                   | Applies on left-hand (even) pages                       |
| `page-right:`                  | Applies on right-hand (odd) pages                       |
| `print:`                       | Always-active (unlike browsers where it's print-preview only) |
| `imprint:cmyk-[c_m_y_k]`       | CMYK colour. Requires `@imprint-pdf/print`              |
| `imprint:spot-[Pantone-185-C]` | Named spot colour                                       |
| `imprint:overprint`            | PDF overprint flag                                      |
| `imprint:bleed-[3mm]`          | Bleed area size                                         |

### Dropped CSS properties (no PDF analogue)

- Interaction pseudo-classes (`hover:`, `focus:`, `active:`, `focus-visible:`)
- Position modes (`position: sticky`, `position: fixed`)
- Overflow/scroll (`overflow: auto`, `overflow: scroll`)
- Transitions/animations (`transition-*`, `animation-*`, `@keyframes`)
- Screen pseudo-selectors (`sm:`, `md:`, `lg:` — treated as always-active)

Pass `{ strict: true }` to `pdf()` to get warnings on dropped properties.

---

## Print-grade output (`@imprint-pdf/print`)

```ts
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/react';
import { printIntent } from '@imprint-pdf/print';
import fogra39 from './icc/FOGRA39.icc';

const response = await pdf(<Doc />, {
  postProcess: [
    printIntent({
      intent: 'PDF/X-4',
      outputIntent: {
        iccProfile: fogra39,
        condition: 'ISO Coated v3',
        conditionIdentifier: 'FOGRA39',
      },
      bleed: '3mm',
      marks: ['trim', 'registration'],
    }),
  ],
});
```

PDF/A-3 with factur-X / ZUGFeRD attachment:

```ts
printIntent({
  intent: 'PDF/A-3B',
  facturX: { level: 'EN 16931', xml: xmlBytes, filename: 'factur-x.xml' },
});
```

---

## Digital signatures (`@imprint-pdf/sign`)

```ts
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/react';
import { signBuffer } from '@imprint-pdf/sign';

const unsigned = await pdf(<Contract data={data} />, { as: 'bytes' });

const signed = await signBuffer(unsigned, {
  certificate: fs.readFileSync('./certs/company.pem', 'utf8'),
  privateKey: fs.readFileSync('./certs/company.key', 'utf8'),
  reason: 'Agreement executed',
  location: 'San Francisco, CA',
  tsaUrl: 'https://freetsa.org/tsr',
});
```

---

## PDF/UA-1 accessibility (`@imprint-pdf/ua`)

```ts
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/react';
import { taggedPdf } from '@imprint-pdf/ua';

const response = await pdf(
  <Document lang="en-US" title="Q1 Report">
    <Page size="A4" className="p-12">
      <h1 className="text-3xl font-bold">Accessible PDF</h1>
      <img src={logoUrl} alt="Acme Corp logo" className="mt-4 h-16" />
    </Page>
  </Document>,
  { postProcess: [taggedPdf()] },
);
```

PDF/UA-1 requires `<Document lang="…" title="…">` and `alt` on every image.

---

## Common patterns

### Multi-page with running header/footer + page numbers

```tsx
<Document>
  <Header>
    <div className="flex justify-between text-xs text-gray-400">
      <span>Acme Corp — Confidential</span>
      <span>Page <PageNumber /> / <TotalPages /></span>
    </div>
  </Header>

  <Page size="A4" className="p-12">{/* page 1 */}</Page>
  <Page size="A4" className="p-12">{/* page 2 */}</Page>
</Document>
```

### Force a page break / avoid breaking inside

```tsx
<h2 className="break-before-page">Chapter 2</h2>
<div className="break-inside-avoid">
  <img …/>
  <p>Caption</p>
</div>
```

### Widows / orphans

```tsx
<p className="[orphans:3] [widows:3]">Long paragraph…</p>
```

Or globally:

```ts
defineConfig({
  typography: { defaultWidows: 3, defaultOrphans: 3 },
});
```

### Variable fonts

```ts
// imprint.config.ts
defineConfig({
  fonts: [{
    family: 'Inter Variable',
    src: './public/fonts/InterVariable.woff2',
    variable: true,
    axes: { wght: [100, 900], slnt: [-10, 0] },
  }],
});
```

```tsx
<p className="[font-variation-settings:'wght'_350]">Light text.</p>
```

### Streaming for large documents

```ts
const stream = await pdf(<LargeReport pages={100} />, { as: 'stream' });
return new Response(stream, { headers: { 'content-type': 'application/pdf' } });
```

First byte in under 50 ms for most documents.

### Batch generation (Node worker thread)

```ts
// worker.ts
import { parentPort, workerData } from 'node:worker_threads';
import { pdf } from '@imprint-pdf/react';
import { Invoice } from './templates/Invoice';

const bytes = await pdf(<Invoice data={workerData} />, { as: 'bytes' });
parentPort!.postMessage(bytes, [bytes.buffer]);
```

---

## CLI

```bash
npx imprint init                    # detect framework, scaffold config + example
npx imprint render <file> -o out/   # render a template to disk
npx imprint dev <file>              # live preview server with HMR (default port 4000)
npx imprint validate <pdf> --profile pdf-ua-1  # veraPDF validation, exits non-zero on failure
```

`imprint init` detects Next.js (App / Pages), Vite, or generic Node and writes:
- `imprint.config.ts`
- `withImprint` wrap on `next.config.{ts,mjs}` OR `imprint()` plugin in `vite.config.ts`
- `templates/ExamplePdf.tsx`
- `app/api/pdf/route.tsx` (Next App), `pages/api/pdf.tsx` (Next Pages), or `src/pdf.ts` (Vite)

---

## Errors and fixes

### `Tailwind compilation failed` / classes resolve to `{}`

Make sure `imprint.config.ts` is at the project root and either `tailwind.stylesheet` (v4) or `tailwind.config` (v3) is set — or that an auto-detectable stylesheet exists at `src/app.css` / `app/globals.css` / etc.

### `RangeError: Index out of range` when loading a font

`@pdf-lib/fontkit@1.1.1`'s WOFF2 decoder rejects some pre-subsetted WOFF2 files (notably Bunny Fonts' Outfit family). Switch to the TTF variant of the font.

### Next 16 / Turbopack renders unstyled

`withImprint()` automatically configures the Turbopack `resolveAlias`. If you've overridden `next.config.ts.turbopack` manually, ensure you preserve `withImprint`'s settings.

### `gstatic` font URL returns 404

Google rotates `fonts.gstatic.com/s/inter/v18/...` slugs (`v15` → `v22`). Use stable hosts:
- Bunny Fonts: `https://fonts.bunny.net/inter/files/inter-latin-400-normal.woff2`
- Fontsource via jsdelivr: `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@fontsource/inter@5/files/inter-latin-400-normal.woff2`
- Self-host from `./public/fonts/`

### `<PageNumber />` renders as empty space when used as a sibling of text

Already handled — components default to `display: 'inline-flex'`. If you've explicitly overridden the display value, set it back to `inline-flex` or use it inside a `<span>` parent.

### `npm install @imprint-pdf/tailwind` fails

This package was unpublished. The Tailwind compiler is bundled inside `@imprint-pdf/react`, `@imprint-pdf/next`, and `@imprint-pdf/vite`. Remove `@imprint-pdf/tailwind` from your `package.json`.

### Deploy: `Cannot find module 'react-reconciler-18'`

Production deploy of a Next.js app on `output: 'standalone'` (Docker, Coolify, self-hosted Vercel). Vercel's nft didn't trace `react-reconciler` into `.next/standalone/node_modules`. Upgrade to `@imprint-pdf/react@1.0.0-alpha.7+` (lazy `await import('react-reconciler-XX')` which nft *can* trace) and use `withImprint()` from `@imprint-pdf/next/plugin`. Pre-alpha.7 used `createRequire(import.meta.url)('react-reconciler-18')` which nft couldn't follow. See [Next.js → Standalone deployments](#standalone-deployments-docker--coolify--self-hosted-vercel).

### Deploy: `Cannot find module 'tailwindcss'`

Same scenario, different module. `tailwindcss` and `postcss` are typically in `devDependencies`, so nft doesn't copy them into standalone. `withImprint()` (≥ `1.0.0-alpha.8`) adds them to `serverExternalPackages` + `experimental.outputFileTracingIncludes` so nft includes them anyway. Alternative: promote both from `devDependencies` → `dependencies`.

### `next build` OOM in `Collecting build traces`

Build's Node process runs out of heap during trace collection. Default Node heap (~4 GB) can be exceeded by large monorepos (imprint-pdf + Sentry + UI lib + ORM client + many routes). Two levers:

1. `ENV NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192` (or 12288) on the builder stage of the Dockerfile.
2. Upgrade to `@imprint-pdf/react@1.0.0-alpha.7+`. Pre-alpha.7 used static `import * as Reconciler18 from 'react-reconciler-18'` which inflated every route's eager bundle. Alpha.7 switched to lazy `await import(...)` so the reconciler lives in a separate async chunk and per-route trace work shrinks.

### Layout: tiny labels overlap their values; badge text appears bottom-aligned

Pre-alpha.6 the Taffy layout pass measured every text leaf at the default 12pt × 1.2 lineHeight regardless of the parent's `text-[6.5pt]` etc., while `drawText` correctly cascaded the parent's fontSize. Layout boxes were sized for 12pt; glyphs were drawn at 6.5pt. Upgrade to `@imprint-pdf/core@1.0.0-alpha.7+`.

---

## Type signatures (quick reference)

```ts
// @imprint-pdf/react
export function pdf(element: ReactElement, options?: PdfOptions): Promise<Response | Uint8Array | ReadableStream<Uint8Array>>;
export function renderToBuffer(element: ReactElement, options?: RenderOptions): Promise<Uint8Array>;
export function renderToStream(element: ReactElement, options?: RenderOptions): Promise<ReadableStream<Uint8Array>>;

// @imprint-pdf/next
export function pdf(element: ReactElement, options?: PdfOptions): Promise<Response | Uint8Array | ReadableStream<Uint8Array>>;
// + @imprint-pdf/next/plugin
export function withImprint(options?: ImprintPluginOptions): (next: NextConfig) => NextConfig;

// @imprint-pdf/vite
export function imprint(options?: ImprintViteOptions): Plugin[];

// @imprint-pdf/core/config
export function defineConfig(config: ImprintConfigInput): ImprintConfig;

// @imprint-pdf/print
export function printIntent(options: PrintIntentOptions): PdfPostProcessHook;

// @imprint-pdf/sign
export function signBuffer(pdf: Uint8Array, options: SignOptions): Promise<Uint8Array>;

// @imprint-pdf/ua
export function taggedPdf(): PdfPostProcessHook;
export function validateUA(document: DocumentNode): { valid: boolean; errors: string[] };
```

---

## License

Apache-2.0, every package. Engine, React layer, Tailwind compiler, CLI, integrations, _and_ the compliance & print add-ons (PDF/X-4 + CMYK + ICC, PDF/UA-1 tagged PDF, PKCS#7 signing, factur-X / ZUGFeRD). No commercial seats, no time-bombed source.

---

## Repository

https://github.com/tamimbinhakim/imprint-pdf
